3.why: count will hold how many intervals are not covered by any other.
3.invariant: No interval has been counted yet.

4.happening: We store n = {len(intervals)}, the number of intervals.
4.why: Both the outer and inner loops range over all n intervals.
4.invariant: n never changes during the algorithm.

5.happening: Outer pointer i = {i} selects the interval we are testing.
5.why: We must check every interval to decide whether some other interval covers it.
5.invariant: Every interval before i has already been decided.

6.happening: We assume interval i is not covered yet.
6.why: covered will flip to True only if we find an interval that covers i.
6.invariant: covered describes only interval i's status this round.

7.happening: We read a = {intervals[i][0]}, interval i's start.
7.why: The covering test needs interval i's own endpoints.
7.invariant: a is interval i's left endpoint.

8.happening: We read b = {intervals[i][1]}, interval i's end.
8.why: Interval i is the half-open range [a, b).
8.invariant: b is interval i's right endpoint.

9.happening: Inner pointer j = {j} scans candidate covering intervals.
9.why: Any other interval in the list might cover interval i.
9.invariant: j visits every index, including i, which is skipped next.

10.happening: j has reached interval i itself.
10.why: An interval is never treated as covering itself here.
10.invariant: We only ever compare two distinct intervals.

10.happening: j ({j}) is a different interval from i ({i}).
10.why: Only other intervals can cover interval i.
10.invariant: i and j point at two distinct intervals here.

11.happening: We skip the self-comparison and advance j.
11.why: Comparing interval i with itself would always look like a cover and is meaningless.
11.invariant: Only distinct pairs of intervals are ever tested.

12.happening: We read c = {intervals[j][0]}, the candidate's start.
12.why: A covering interval must start at or before a.
12.invariant: c is interval j's left endpoint.

13.happening: We read d = {intervals[j][1]}, the candidate's end.
13.why: A covering interval must end at or after b.
13.invariant: d is interval j's right endpoint.

14.happening: c ({c}) <= a ({a}) and b ({b}) <= d ({d}) — interval j covers interval i.
14.why: This is the exact definition: [a, b) is covered by [c, d) when c <= a and b <= d.
14.invariant: A covered interval will not be added to count.

14.happening: Interval j (start {c}, end {d}) does not cover interval i (start {a}, end {b}).
14.why: At least one of c <= a or b <= d fails, so j cannot swallow interval i.
14.invariant: covered stays False until some interval covers i.

15.happening: We mark interval i as covered.
15.why: We found an interval j whose range fully contains interval i.
15.invariant: Once covered is True, interval i will not be counted.

16.happening: We stop the inner scan; one covering interval is enough.
16.why: There is no need to look further once interval i is known to be covered.
16.invariant: The inner loop ends early for a covered interval.

17.happening: No interval covered interval i, so we keep it.
17.why: An interval survives only if nothing in the list covers it.
17.invariant: Kept intervals are covered by no other interval.

17.happening: Interval i was covered, so we do not count it.
17.why: Covered intervals are removed from the answer.
17.invariant: count grows only for surviving intervals.

18.happening: Interval i survived, so count becomes {count + 1}.
18.why: It passed the test: no other interval covers it.
18.invariant: count equals the number of surviving intervals so far.

19.why: Every interval has now been tested against all the others.
19.invariant: count is the number of intervals not covered by any other.

init.happening: Set up the counter and interval count.
init.why: The algorithm needs a survivor counter and the total number of intervals.
init.invariant: No interval has been tested yet.

loop.happening: Advance one of the two pointers over the intervals.
loop.why: Every interval is tested against every other interval.
loop.invariant: The pair (i, j) describes the two intervals currently compared.

update.happening: Read interval endpoints or record a decision.
update.why: Each comparison needs the endpoints of both intervals.
update.invariant: State always reflects the concrete pair being examined.

check.happening: Test whether one interval covers another.
check.why: An interval is removed exactly when some other interval covers it.

move.happening: Skip or break out of the inner scan.
move.why: We ignore self-comparisons and stop early once a cover is found.
move.invariant: The inner loop only spends work on useful comparisons.

return.invariant: count is the number of intervals not covered by any other.
